jobs.title: Sr. Engineer, Process Systems (Contract)
jobs.description: Join our Mission to Protect Humankind! Vaxcyte is a clinical-stage vaccine innovation company engineering high-fidelity vaccines to protect humankind from the consequences of bacterial diseases, which have serious and costly health consequences when left unchecked. Working to eradicate or treat bacterial infections such as invasive pneumococcal disease, Group A Strep, and Shigella is just the beginning. Our path to success is clear and well-defined, and Vaxcyte is set up to go the distance. WHAT we do is every bit as important as HOW we do it! Our work together is guided by four enduring core values: *RETHINK CONVENTION: We bring creative and intellectual diversity to every facet of the work we do in order to innovate and re-innovate the way vaccines are delivered. *AIM HIGH: We embody our collectively audacious goal to courageously make the most complex biologics ever attempted to protect humankind. *LEAD WITH HEART: Everyone leads at Vaxcyte with a kindness-first, inclusive approach to collaboration and vigorous debate that advances our business objectives. *MODEL EXCELLENCE: The magnitude of our challenge requires our shared commitment to demonstrating integrity, accountability, equality and clarity across communications and decision making. ## Summary: As Vaxcyte’s products progress towards late stage and commercialization, Vaxcyte is implementing dedicated biologics manufacturing facilities. This contract position will support all aspects of process systems engineering activities related to commercial launch, and routine GMP manufacturing to ensure the efficient and compliant production of Vaxcyte's products. The Sr. Engineer, Process Systems (Contract) will work closely with Process Development, MSAT, Supply Chain, Quality, Project Management, Business Development, and Regulatory functions, as well as CDMOs. This role is responsible for leading and overseeing process systems-related activities for biologics manufacturing facilities, systems, and equipment. As a technical leader within the field, the Sr. Engineer, Process Systems (Contract) ensures that all processes and systems comply with regulatory and company standards, contributes to the development of company concepts & principles and solves complex problems in creative and effective ways. The role involves collaborating with cross-functional teams to support new installations, system upgrades, and ongoing operations. Effective cross-functional teamwork and successful engagement with CDMOs are critical to success in this position. This position will report to the Director of Engineering & Facilities at Vaxcyte. ## Essential Functions: ## Process System Owner: - Lead and manage all engineering activities for advanced biotechnological systems, including utilities, process equipment, automation systems, and facilities. - Work on significant and unique issues where analysis of situations or data requires an evaluation of intangible variables and may impact future concepts, products or technologies. - Review and approve process equipment documentation, such as protocols, test plans, reports, and other deliverables, ensuring alignment with company standards and regulatory requirements. - Conduct comprehensive risk assessments and design strategies to validate and optimize critical process parameters in compliance with industry standards and regulatory guidelines. - Lead complex system and equipment inspections, including FATs (Factory Acceptance Tests), SATs (Site Acceptance Tests), and IQ/OQ/PQ (Installation Qualification/Operational Qualification/Performance Qualification), ensuring precision and compliance. - Lead engineering feasibility studies, conceptual & basic design activities for projects across a range of products and sites. ## Validation: - Direct and manage validation initiatives, ensuring compliance with cGMP, FDA, EMA, and other applicable regulatory agencies. - Oversee the review and approval of validation protocols, plans, and reports, ensuring their alignment with industry standards and ensuring robust and reliable processes. - Analyze complex validation data to identify critical trends, continuously improving the reliability, efficiency, and compliance of validated systems. ## Project Management: - Create formal networks with key decision makers and interface with CDMO and supplier partners. - Oversees the activities of a project and teams involved in cross-functional engineering projects from conception through completion, balancing complex timelines, budget constraints, resource allocation, and quality standards. - Collaborate effectively with senior project managers, cross-functional teams, contractors, and external vendors to coordinate, prioritize, and execute engineering activities. - Ensure project deliverables are met within scope and timeline while maintaining focus on compliance, quality, and cost-effectiveness. ## Regulatory Compliance: - Ensure full regulatory compliance of all systems with FDA, EMA, and other global regulatory requirements. - Lead and support regulatory inspections, audits, and customer site visits, including presenting documentation and addressing regulatory concerns with senior leadership. - Monitor, interpret, and incorporate changes in regulatory guidelines, ensuring Vaxcyte’s engineering processes are continuously aligned with evolving standards. ## Leadership and Strategy: - Exercise latitude in determining objectives and approaches to critical assignments. - Drive strategic improvements in engineering processes, tools, and methodologies, ensuring the continuous optimization of systems and compliance practices across the organization. - Collaborate with senior leadership to define long-term engineering strategies and goals, ensuring alignment with organizational objectives and growth plans. - Lead the transition from capital project implementation to ongoing engineering support for commercial manufacturing, emphasizing preventive maintenance, continuous improvement, equipment upgrades, and the integration of evolving industry standards to ensure operational efficiency and compliance. - Take accountability for complex projects, scopes of work and technical troubleshooting. - Drive strategic alignment, standardization and adoption of engineering lifecycle management programs with CDMOs and partners. ## Requirements: Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Engineering (Chemical, Mechanical, Biomedical, or related field) or Biotechnology with a minimum of 10+ years of experience in Bioprocessing/Process Engineering within the biotechnology, pharmaceutical, or biopharmaceutical industry. - Proven leadership experience in managing teams and large-scale Capex projects, including utilities, process equipment, and automation systems. - Extensive knowledge of biopharmaceutical manufacturing processes and equipment, particularly DCS, ISA-88 batch process control, and PLC-based control systems. - Demonstrated success in managing engineering activities for large-scale or complex facilities under tight timelines and budget constraints. - Expert-level knowledge of cGMP, FDA, EMA, ICH guidelines, and other global regulatory requirements. - Strong understanding of bioprocessing, including upstream and downstream processing, aseptic processing, and sterilization techniques. - Advanced proficiency in statistical analysis, risk assessment methodologies, and process validation best practices. - In-depth experience with quality management systems (QMS) and electronic documentation systems to maintain compliant documentation and support audit activities. - Capability to support and, when required, perform a range of broader engineering functions and activities, including project management, process optimization, automation, and utilities engineering. - Exceptional leadership, communication, and interpersonal skills, with the ability to influence at all levels of the organization and external partners. - Strong problem-solving, analytical thinking, and decision-making capabilities, especially in high-pressure environments. - Ability to prioritize and manage multiple complex projects, balancing conflicting demands while ensuring regulatory compliance and business goals. Reports to: Director of Engineering & Facilities Location: Visp, Valais, Switzerland Expected Contract Length: 12 Months We are an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability or veteran status.

descriptionByLocale.it: Unisciti alla nostra missione per proteggere l'umanità! Vaxcyte è una società di innovazione vaccinale in fase clinica che progetta vaccini ad alta fedeltà per proteggere l'umanità dalle conseguenze delle malattie batteriche, che hanno conseguenze gravi e costose sulla salute se non controllate. Lavorare per sradicare o trattare le infezioni batteriche come la malattia pneumococcica invasiva, lo streptococco di gruppo A e la shigella è solo l'inizio. Il nostro percorso verso il successo è chiaro e ben definito e Vaxcyte è impostato per andare lontano. QUELLO che facciamo è tanto importante quanto il MODO in cui lo facciamo! Il nostro lavoro insieme è guidato da quattro valori fondamentali duraturi: *RIPENSARE LA CONVENZIONE: portiamo la diversità creativa e intellettuale in ogni aspetto del lavoro che facciamo per innovare e reinnovare il modo in cui vengono consegnati i vaccini. *PUNTARE IN ALTO: Incarniamo il nostro obiettivo collettivamente audace di rendere coraggiosamente i biologici più complessi mai tentati di proteggere l'umanità. *GUIDA CON IL CUORE: tutti GUIDANO Vaxcyte con un approccio inclusivo e basato sulla gentilezza alla collaborazione e un dibattito vigoroso che promuove i nostri obiettivi aziendali. * ECCELLENZA DEL MODELLO: L'entità della nostra sfida richiede il nostro impegno condiviso a dimostrare integrità, responsabilità, uguaglianza e chiarezza nelle comunicazioni e nel processo decisionale. ## Riepilogo: man mano che i prodotti di Vaxcyte progrediscono verso la fase avanzata e la commercializzazione, Vaxcyte sta implementando impianti di produzione biologici dedicati. Questa posizione contrattuale supporterà tutti gli aspetti dei sistemi di processo - Garantire che i risultati del progetto siano soddisfatti entro l'ambito e la tempistica, mantenendo al contempo l'attenzione sulla conformità, la qualità e l'economicità. ## Conformità normativa: - Garantire la piena conformità normativa di tutti i sistemi con FDA, EMA e altri requisiti normativi globali. - Guidare e supportare le ispezioni normative, gli audit e le visite in loco dei clienti, compresa la presentazione della documentazione e la risoluzione dei problemi normativi con la leadership senior. - Monitora, interpreta e incorpora i cambiamenti nelle linee guida normative, assicurando che i processi di ingegneria di Vaxcyte siano continuamente allineati con gli standard in evoluzione. ## Leadership e strategia: - Esercitare libertà nella determinazione degli obiettivi e degli approcci agli incarichi critici. - Promuovere miglioramenti strategici nei processi, negli strumenti e nelle metodologie di ingegneria, garantendo la continua ottimizzazione dei sistemi e delle pratiche di conformità in tutta l'organizzazione. - Collaborare con la leadership senior per definire strategie e obiettivi di ingegneria a lungo termine, garantendo l'allineamento con gli obiettivi organizzativi e i piani di crescita. - Guidare la transizione dall'implementazione di progetti di capitale al supporto tecnico continuo per la produzione commerciale, sottolineando la manutenzione preventiva, il miglioramento continuo, gli aggiornamenti delle apparecchiature e l'integrazione di standard industriali in evoluzione per garantire efficienza operativa e conformità. - Assumersi la responsabilità di progetti complessi, ambiti di lavoro e risoluzione dei problemi tecnici. - Guidare l'allineamento strategico, la standardizzazione e
descriptionByLocale.de: Treten Sie unserer Mission zum Schutz der Menschheit bei! Vaxcyte ist ein Unternehmen für Impfstoffinnovationen im klinischen Stadium, das High-Fidelity-Impfstoffe entwickelt, um die Menschheit vor den Folgen bakterieller Krankheiten zu schützen, die schwerwiegende und kostspielige gesundheitliche Folgen haben, wenn sie nicht kontrolliert werden. Die Beseitigung oder Behandlung von bakteriellen Infektionen wie invasiven Pneumokokken-Erkrankungen, Streptokokken der Gruppe A und Shigellen ist nur der Anfang. Unser Weg zum Erfolg ist klar und klar definiert, und Vaxcyte ist so eingestellt, dass es die Strecke geht. WAS wir tun, ist genauso wichtig wie die ART UND WEISE, WIE wir es tun! Unsere gemeinsame Arbeit wird von vier dauerhaften Grundwerten geleitet: * Rethink CONVENTION: Wir bringen kreative und intellektuelle Vielfalt in jede Facette unserer Arbeit, um die Art und Weise, wie Impfstoffe verabreicht werden, zu erneuern und zu erneuern. *AIM HIGH: Wir verkörpern unser kollektiv kühnes Ziel, mutig die komplexesten Biologika herzustellen, die jemals versucht wurden, die Menschheit zu schützen. *FÜHREN SIE MIT HERZ: Bei Vaxcyte führt jeder mit einem freundlichen, integrativen Ansatz für die Zusammenarbeit und einer lebhaften Debatte, die unsere Geschäftsziele voranbringt. *MODEL EXCELLENCE: Das Ausmaß unserer Herausforderung erfordert unser gemeinsames Engagement, Integrität, Rechenschaftspflicht, Gleichheit und Klarheit in der Kommunikation und Entscheidungsfindung zu demonstrieren. ## Zusammenfassung: Während sich die Produkte von Vaxcyte in Richtung Spätstadium und Kommerzialisierung entwickeln, implementiert Vaxcyte spezielle Produktionsstätten für Biologika. Diese Vertragsposition wird alle Aspekte von Prozesssystemen unterstützen - Stellen Sie sicher, dass die Projektergebnisse innerhalb des Umfangs und des Zeitplans eingehalten werden, und konzentrieren Sie sich gleichzeitig auf Compliance, Qualität und Kosteneffizienz. ## Einhaltung behördlicher Auflagen: - Sicherstellung der vollständigen Einhaltung der FDA-, EMA- und anderer globaler behördlicher Auflagen durch alle Systeme. - Leitung und Unterstützung von behördlichen Inspektionen, Audits und Kundenbesuchen vor Ort, einschließlich der Vorlage von Unterlagen und der Auseinandersetzung mit behördlichen Bedenken mit der Geschäftsleitung. - Überwachung, Interpretation und Einbeziehung von Änderungen der regulatorischen Richtlinien, um sicherzustellen, dass die Engineering-Prozesse von Vaxcyte kontinuierlich an den sich entwickelnden Standards ausgerichtet sind. ## Führung und Strategie: - Üben Sie Spielraum bei der Festlegung von Zielen und Ansätzen für kritische Aufgaben. - Förderung strategischer Verbesserungen von Engineering-Prozessen, -Tools und -Methoden, um die kontinuierliche Optimierung von Systemen und Compliance-Praktiken im gesamten Unternehmen sicherzustellen. - Zusammenarbeit mit der Geschäftsleitung, um langfristige Engineering-Strategien und -Ziele zu definieren und die Ausrichtung an den Unternehmenszielen und Wachstumsplänen sicherzustellen. - Leitung des Übergangs von der Implementierung von Investitionsprojekten zur laufenden technischen Unterstützung für die kommerzielle Fertigung, wobei der Schwerpunkt auf präventiver Wartung, kontinuierlicher Verbesserung, Geräte-Upgrades und der Integration sich entwickelnder Industriestandards liegt, um die betriebliche Effizienz und Compliance zu gewährleisten. - Übernahme der Verantwortung für komplexe Projekte, Arbeitsumfänge und technische Fehlerbehebung. - Förderung der strategischen Ausrichtung, Standardisierung und
descriptionByLocale.fr: Rejoignez notre mission de protection de l'humanité ! Vaxcyte est une société d'innovation en matière de vaccins au stade clinique qui conçoit des vaccins haute fidélité pour protéger l'humanité des conséquences des maladies bactériennes, qui ont des conséquences graves et coûteuses sur la santé lorsqu'elles ne sont pas contrôlées. Travailler à éradiquer ou à traiter les infections bactériennes telles que la pneumococcie invasive, le streptocoque du groupe A et la shigella n'est que le début. Notre chemin vers le succès est clair et bien défini, et Vaxcyte est prêt à aller jusqu'au bout. CE que nous faisons est tout aussi important que la FAÇON DONT nous le faisons ! Notre travail ensemble est guidé par quatre valeurs fondamentales durables : *REPENSER LA CONVENTION : Nous apportons une diversité créative et intellectuelle à toutes les facettes de notre travail afin d'innover et de réinventer la façon dont les vaccins sont délivrés. *AIM HIGH : Nous incarnons notre objectif collectivement audacieux de fabriquer courageusement les produits biologiques les plus complexes jamais tentés pour protéger l'humanité. *DIRIGER AVEC CŒUR : Tout le monde dirige chez Vaxcyte avec gentillesse, une approche inclusive de la collaboration et un débat vigoureux qui fait progresser nos objectifs commerciaux. * EXCELLENCE DU MODÈLE : L'ampleur de notre défi nécessite notre engagement commun à faire preuve d'intégrité, de responsabilité, d'égalité et de clarté dans les communications et la prise de décision. ## Résumé : Au fur et à mesure que les produits de Vaxcyte progressent vers un stade avancé et la commercialisation, Vaxcyte met en place des installations de fabrication de produits biologiques dédiées. Ce poste contractuel soutiendra tous les aspects des systèmes de processus - S'assurer que les livrables du projet sont respectés dans les limites de la portée et du calendrier tout en continuant à mettre l'accent sur la conformité, la qualité et la rentabilité. ## Conformité réglementaire : - Assurer la conformité réglementaire complète de tous les systèmes avec la FDA, l'EMA et d'autres exigences réglementaires mondiales. - Diriger et soutenir les inspections réglementaires, les audits et les visites des sites des clients, y compris la présentation de la documentation et la résolution des problèmes réglementaires avec la haute direction. - Surveiller, interpréter et intégrer les changements dans les directives réglementaires, en veillant à ce que les processus d'ingénierie de Vaxcyte soient continuellement alignés sur les normes en évolution. ## Leadership et stratégie : - Exercer une latitude dans la détermination des objectifs et des approches des missions critiques. - Favoriser des améliorations stratégiques dans les processus, les outils et les méthodologies d'ingénierie, en assurant l'optimisation continue des systèmes et des pratiques de conformité dans l'ensemble de l'organisation. - Collaborer avec la haute direction pour définir des stratégies et des objectifs d'ingénierie à long terme, en veillant à l'alignement avec les objectifs organisationnels et les plans de croissance. - Diriger la transition de la mise en œuvre des projets d'investissement au soutien technique continu pour la fabrication commerciale, en mettant l'accent sur la maintenance préventive, l'amélioration continue, les mises à niveau des équipements et l'intégration des normes industrielles en évolution pour assurer l'efficacité opérationnelle et la conformité. - Assumer la responsabilité de projets complexes, de la portée des travaux et du dépannage technique. - Favoriser l'alignement stratégique, la normalisation et

jobs.description: Join our Mission to Protect Humankind! Vaxcyte is a clinical-stage vaccine innovation company engineering high-fidelity vaccines to protect humankind from the consequences of bacterial diseases, which have serious and costly health consequences when left unchecked. Working to eradicate or treat bacterial infections such as invasive pneumococcal disease, Group A Strep, and Shigella is just the beginning. Our path to success is clear and well-defined, and Vaxcyte is set up to go the distance. WHAT we do is every bit as important as HOW we do it! Our work together is guided by four enduring core values: *RETHINK CONVENTION: We bring creative and intellectual diversity to every facet of the work we do in order to innovate and re-innovate the way vaccines are delivered. *AIM HIGH: We embody our collectively audacious goal to courageously make the most complex biologics ever attempted to protect humankind. *LEAD WITH HEART: Everyone leads at Vaxcyte with a kindness-first, inclusive approach to collaboration and vigorous debate that advances our business objectives. *MODEL EXCELLENCE: The magnitude of our challenge requires our shared commitment to demonstrating integrity, accountability, equality and clarity across communications and decision making. ## Summary: The Manager, Supply Chain Management is responsible for day‑to‑day operational execution of supply chain activities supporting Vaxcyte’s clinical, GMP, PPQ, and commercial readiness programs. This role focuses on tactical planning, execution, tracking, and issue resolution across materials, logistics, and external partners, ensuring smooth and compliant operations in support of manufacturing campaigns. The role works closely with the Associate Director, Supply Chain, who retains end‑to‑end strategic ownership across programs, while the Manager, Supply Chain Management ensures disciplined execution, follow‑through, and operational stability. ## Essential Functions: ## Operational Supply Chain Execution - Execute approved supply chain strategies and plans across GMP, PPQ, and clinical/commercial readiness activities. - Manage day‑to‑day material planning, ordering, and tracking to ensure on‑time availability aligned with manufacturing schedules. - Support execution of supply activities across multiple programs, with a focus on operational readiness and delivery. - Manage and coordinate Critical Raw Materials and Customer‑Supplied Materials provided to Lonza. ## Materials, Logistics & Vendor Coordination - Coordinate shipments and storage with the Logistics Team. - Track material movements, inventories, and material status, proactively escalating risks or delays, particularly for Critical Raw Materials. - Act as the primary operational interface between Supply Chain and Procurement / Buyer, ensuring alignment between ordered materials, delivery timelines, and campaign execution needs. ## CDMO & External Partner Support - Support operational interfaces with CDMOs, primarily Lonza. - Participate in recurring Lonza‑related forums (e.g. VIBEX, MINT material ordering, allocation, and operational alignment meetings). - Follow up on action items, timelines, and required documentation to enable smooth execution. - Support deviation‑related activities by coordinating information flow between Supply Chain, Quality, and Operations. ## Tracking, Reporting & Documentation - Maintain operational trackers, dashboards, and status updates related to materials, shipments, and readiness. - Own the accuracy and maintenance of campaign progression data, including OMP‑related tracking, ensuring consistency across Supply Chain, Manufacturing, and Lonza‑reported data. - Support preparation of weekly and recurring campaign updates by providing validated execution‑level data. ## Issue Resolution & Continuous Improvement - Act as a first line of response for operational issues related to materials, logistics, and execution. - Escalate issues with clear data, options, and recommendations to the Associate Director. - Identify opportunities to improve execution efficiency, tracking, and operational robustness. ## Requirements: Bachelor’s degree in Supply Chain, Operations, Engineering, Life Sciences, or a related field with 7+ years of experience in Supply Chain Operations, Manufacturing Support, or a related role. Other combinations of education and/or experience may be considered. - Experience working in a regulated biotech, pharmaceutical, or life sciences environment. - Strong ability to work in a cross‑functional, matrix organization. - Hands‑on experience with material planning, logistics coordination, and operational execution. - Strong analytical, organizational, and follow‑through skills. - Proficiency with ERP systems, SharePoint, and MS Office tools. - Fluent in English. - Fluency in German highly preferred; other European languages an advantage. Reports to: Associate Director, Supply Chain Location: Visp, Switzerland (hybrid) or Steinhausen, ZG, Switzerland (hybrid) Compensation: The compensation package will be competitive and includes comprehensive benefits and an equity component. #LI-JN1 We are an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability or veteran status.

Act as the primary operational interface between Supply Chain and Procurement / Buyer, ensuring alignment between ordered materials, delivery timelines, and campaign execution needs. ## CDMO & External Partner Support - Support operational interfaces with CDMOs, primarily Lonza. - Participate in recurring Lonza‑related forums (e.g. VIBEX, MINT material ordering, allocation, and operational alignment meetings). - Follow up on action items, timelines, and required documentation to enable smooth execution. - Support deviation‑related activities by coordinating information flow between Supply Chain, Quality, and Operations. ## Tracking, Reporting & Documentation - Maintain operational trackers, dashboards, and status updates related to materials, shipments, and readiness. - Own the accuracy and maintenance of campaign progression data, including OMP‑related tracking, ensuring consistency across Supply Chain, Manufacturing, and Lonza‑reported data. - Support preparation of weekly and recurring campaign updates by providing validated execution‑level data. ## Issue Resolution & Continuous Improvement - Act as a first line of response for operational issues related to materials, logistics, and execution. - Escalate issues with clear data, options, and recommendations to the Associate Director. - Identify opportunities to improve execution efficiency, tracking, and operational robustness. ## Requirements: Bachelor’s degree in Supply Chain, Operations, Engineering, Life Sciences, or a related field with 7+ years of experience in Supply Chain Operations, Manufacturing Support, or a related role. Other combinations of education and/or experience may be considered. - Experience working in a regulated biotech, pharmaceutical, or life sciences environment. - Strong ability to work in a cross‑functional, matrix organization. - Hands‑on experience with material planning, logistics coordination, and operational execution. - Strong analytical, organizational, and follow‑through skills. - Proficiency with ERP systems, SharePoint, and MS Office tools. - Fluent in English. - Fluency in German highly preferred; other European languages an advantage. Reports to: Associate Director, Supply Chain Location: Visp, Switzerland (hybrid) or Steinhausen, ZG, Switzerland (hybrid) Compensation: The compensation package will be competitive and includes comprehensive benefits and an equity component. #LI-JN1 We are an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability or veteran status.
descriptionByLocale.it: Unisciti alla nostra missione per proteggere l'umanità! Vaxcyte è un'azienda innovativa nel campo dei vaccini in fase clinica, che sviluppa vaccini ad alta fedeltà per proteggere l'umanità dalle conseguenze delle malattie batteriche, che possono avere gravi e costosi impatti sulla salute se non controllate. Lavorare per eradicare o trattare infezioni batteriche come la malattia pneumococcica invasiva, lo Streptococco di gruppo A e la Shigella è solo l'inizio. Il nostro percorso verso il successo è chiaro e ben definito, e Vaxcyte è pronta a percorrerlo fino in fondo. COSA facciamo è importante tanto quanto COME lo facciamo! Il nostro lavoro è guidato da quattro valori fondamentali e duraturi: *RIPENSARE LA CONVENZIONE: Portiamo diversità creativa e intellettuale in ogni aspetto del nostro lavoro per innovare e reinventare il modo in cui i vaccini vengono forniti. *MIRARE IN ALTO: Incarniamo il nostro obiettivo collettivo e audace di creare i biologici più complessi mai tentati per proteggere l'umanità. *GUIDARE CON IL CUORE: Tutti in Vaxcyte guidano con un approccio inclusivo e orientato alla gentilezza nella collaborazione e nel dibattito vigoroso che avanza i nostri obiettivi aziendali. *MODELLARE L'ECCELLENZA: La magnitudine della nostra sfida richiede un impegno condiviso per dimostrare integrità, responsabilità, uguaglianza e chiarezza nella comunicazione e nel processo decisionale. ## Riassunto: Il Responsabile della Gestione della Catena di Fornitura è responsabile dell'esecuzione operativa quotidiana delle attività della catena di fornitura a supporto dei programmi clinici, GMP, PPQ e di preparazione commerciale di Vaxcyte. Questo ruolo si concentra sulla pianificazione tattica, l'esecuzione, il monitoraggio e la risoluzione dei problemi relativi a materiali, logistica e partner esterni, garantendo operazioni fluide e conformi a supporto delle campagne di produzione. Il ruolo collabora strettamente con il Direttore Associato della Catena di Fornitura, che mantiene la proprietà strategica end-to-end dei programmi, mentre il Responsabile della Gestione della Catena di Fornitura garantisce un'esecuzione disciplinata, il follow-through e la stabilità operativa. ## Funzioni Essenziali: ### Esecuzione Operativa della Catena di Fornitura - Eseguire strategie e piani approvati per la catena di fornitura relativi ad attività GMP, PPQ e di preparazione clinica/commerciale. - Gestire la pianificazione, l'ordinazione e il monitoraggio quotidiani dei materiali per garantire la disponibilità puntuale in linea con i programmi di produzione. - Supportare l'esecuzione delle attività di fornitura attraverso più programmi, con un focus sulla prontezza operativa e la consegna. - Gestire e coordinare Materie Prime Critiche e Materiali Forniti dal Cliente forniti a Lonza. ### Coordinamento Materiali, Logistica e Fornitori - Coordinare spedizioni e stoccaggio con il Team Logistica. - Monitorare i movimenti dei materiali, gli inventari e lo stato dei materiali, segnalando proattivamente rischi o ritardi, in particolare per le Materie Prime Critiche. - Agire come interfaccia operativa principale tra Catena di Fornitura e Acquisti/Buyer, garantendo l'allineamento tra i materiali ordinati, le tempistiche di consegna e le esigenze di esecuzione delle campagne. ### Supporto a CDMO e Partner Esterni - Supportare le interfacce operative con i CDMO, principalmente Lonza. - Partecipare a forum ricorrenti relativi a Lonza (ad esempio VIBEX, riunioni di ordinazione materiali MINT, allocazione e allineamento operativo). - Seguire le azioni, le tempistiche e la documentazione richiesta per consentire un'esecuzione fluida. - Supportare le attività relative alle deviazioni coordinando il flusso di informazioni tra Catena di Fornitura, Qualità e Operazioni. ### Monitoraggio, Reportistica e Documentazione - Mantenere tracker operativi, dashboard e aggiornamenti di stato relativi a materiali, spedizioni e prontezza. - Possedere l'accuratezza e la manutenzione dei dati di progressione delle campagne, inclusi i tracker OMP, garantendo coerenza tra Catena di Fornitura, Produzione e dati riportati da Lonza. - Supportare la preparazione di aggiornamenti settimanali e ricorrenti delle campagne fornendo dati di esecuzione validati. ### Risoluzione dei Problemi e Miglioramento Continuo - Agire come prima linea di risposta per problemi operativi relativi a materiali, logistica ed esecuzione. - Segnalare problemi con dati chiari, opzioni e raccomandazioni al Direttore Associato. - Identificare opportunità per migliorare l'efficienza dell'esecuzione, il monitoraggio e la robustezza operativa. ## Requisiti: - Laurea in Gestione della Catena di Fornitura, Operazioni, Ingegneria, Scienze della Vita o un campo correlato con 7+ anni di esperienza in Operazioni della Catena di Fornitura, Supporto alla Produzione o un ruolo correlato. Altre combinazioni di istruzione e/o esperienza possono essere considerate. - Esperienza in un ambiente regolamentato di biotecnologia, farmaceutico o scienze della vita. - Forte capacità di lavorare in un'organizzazione trasversale e a matrice. - Esperienza pratica con pianificazione dei materiali, coordinamento logistico ed esecuzione operativa. - Forti capacità analitiche, organizzative e di follow-through. - Competenza con sistemi ERP, SharePoint e strumenti MS Office. - Fluente in inglese. - La conoscenza del tedesco è altamente preferita; altre lingue europee sono un vantaggio. Riporta a: Direttore Associato, Catena di Fornitura Sede: Visp, Svizzera (ibrido) o Steinhausen, ZG, Svizzera (ibrido) Compenso: Il pacchetto retributivo sarà competitivo e include benefici completi e una componente azionaria. #LI-JN1 Siamo un datore di lavoro che offre pari opportunità. Tutti i candidati qualificati riceveranno considerazione per l'impiego senza distinzione di razza, colore, religione, sesso, orientamento sessuale, identità di genere, origine nazionale, disabilità o status di veterano.
descriptionByLocale.de: Schließen Sie sich unserer Mission an, die Menschheit zu schützen! Vaxcyte ist ein Unternehmen für Impfstoffinnovationen in der klinischen Phase, das hochpräzise Impfstoffe entwickelt, um die Menschheit vor den Folgen bakterieller Erkrankungen zu schützen, die bei Nichtbehandlung schwerwiegende und kostspielige gesundheitliche Folgen haben können. Die Bekämpfung oder Behandlung bakterieller Infektionen wie invasiver Pneumokokken-Erkrankungen, Gruppe-A-Streptokokken und Shigella ist erst der Anfang. Unser Weg zum Erfolg ist klar und gut definiert, und Vaxcyte ist bereit, diesen Weg zu gehen. WAS wir tun, ist genauso wichtig wie WIE wir es tun! Unsere Arbeit wird von vier dauerhaften Kernwerten geleitet: *TRADITIONEN ÜBERDENKEN: Wir bringen kreative und intellektuelle Vielfalt in jeden Aspekt unserer Arbeit ein, um die Art und Weise, wie Impfstoffe bereitgestellt werden, zu innovieren und neu zu gestalten. *HOHE ZIELE SETZEN: Wir verkörpern unser gemeinsames, mutiges Ziel, die komplexesten Biologika zu entwickeln, die jemals versucht wurden, um die Menschheit zu schützen. *MIT HERZ FÜHREN: Jeder bei Vaxcyte führt mit einem freundlichen, inklusiven Ansatz zur Zusammenarbeit und lebhaften Diskussionen, die unsere Geschäftsziele voranbringen. *EXZELLENZ VORBILDEN: Das Ausmaß unserer Herausforderung erfordert unser gemeinsames Engagement, Integrität, Verantwortlichkeit, Gleichheit und Klarheit in der Kommunikation und Entscheidungsfindung zu demonstrieren. ## Zusammenfassung: Der Manager für Lieferkettenmanagement ist verantwortlich für die tägliche operative Umsetzung von Lieferkettenaktivitäten zur Unterstützung der klinischen, GMP-, PPQ- und kommerziellen Vorbereitungsprogramme von Vaxcyte. Diese Rolle konzentriert sich auf taktische Planung, Ausführung, Nachverfolgung und Problemlösung in den Bereichen Materialien, Logistik und externe Partner, um reibungslose und konforme Abläufe zur Unterstützung von Produktionskampagnen sicherzustellen. Die Rolle arbeitet eng mit dem Associate Director für Lieferketten zusammen, der die strategische Gesamtverantwortung für die Programme trägt, während der Manager für Lieferkettenmanagement eine disziplinierte Ausführung, Nachverfolgung und operative Stabilität sicherstellt. ## Wesentliche Funktionen: ### Operative Umsetzung der Lieferkette - Umsetzung genehmigter Strategien und Pläne für die Lieferkette in Bezug auf GMP-, PPQ- und klinische/kommerzielle Vorbereitungsaktivitäten. - Verwaltung der täglichen Materialplanung, Bestellung und Nachverfolgung, um die rechtzeitige Verfügbarkeit gemäß den Produktionsplänen sicherzustellen. - Unterstützung der Durchführung von Lieferaktivitäten über mehrere Programme hinweg mit Fokus auf operative Bereitschaft und Lieferung. - Verwaltung und Koordination von Kritischen Rohstoffen und vom Kunden bereitgestellten Materialien, die an Lonza geliefert werden. ### Material-, Logistik- und Lieferantenkoordination - Koordination von Sendungen und Lagerung mit dem Logistikteam. - Nachverfolgung von Materialbewegungen, Beständen und Materialstatus, proaktive Eskalation von Risiken oder Verzögerungen, insbesondere bei Kritischen Rohstoffen. - Hauptschnittstelle zwischen Lieferkette und Beschaffung/Einkauf, um die Abstimmung zwischen bestellten Materialien, Lieferzeiten und Kampagnendurchführungsanforderungen sicherzustellen. ### Unterstützung von CDMOs und externen Partnern - Unterstützung der operativen Schnittstellen mit CDMOs, hauptsächlich Lonza. - Teilnahme an wiederkehrenden Lonza-bezogenen Foren (z. B. VIBEX, MINT-Materialbestellung, Zuweisung und operative Abstimmungsmeetings). - Nachverfolgung von Maßnahmen, Zeitplänen und erforderlicher Dokumentation, um eine reibungslose Durchführung zu ermöglichen. - Unterstützung bei abweichungsbezogenen Aktivitäten durch Koordination des Informationsflusses zwischen Lieferkette, Qualität und Betrieb. ### Nachverfolgung, Berichterstattung und Dokumentation - Pflege von operativen Trackern, Dashboards und Statusaktualisierungen in Bezug auf Materialien, Sendungen und Bereitschaft. - Verantwortung für die Genauigkeit und Pflege der Kampagnenfortschrittsdaten, einschließlich OMP-bezogener Nachverfolgung, um Konsistenz zwischen Lieferkette, Produktion und von Lonza gemeldeten Daten sicherzustellen. - Unterstützung bei der Vorbereitung wöchentlicher und wiederkehrender Kampagnenaktualisierungen durch Bereitstellung validierter Ausführungsdaten. ### Problemlösung und kontinuierliche Verbesserung - Erste Anlaufstelle für operative Probleme im Zusammenhang mit Materialien, Logistik und Ausführung. - Eskalation von Problemen mit klaren Daten, Optionen und Empfehlungen an den Associate Director. - Identifizierung von Möglichkeiten zur Verbesserung der Ausführungseffizienz, Nachverfolgung und operativen Robustheit. ## Anforderungen: - Bachelor-Abschluss in Lieferkettenmanagement, Betrieb, Ingenieurwesen, Lebenswissenschaften oder einem verwandten Bereich mit 7+ Jahren Erfahrung in Lieferkettenoperationen, Produktionsunterstützung oder einer verwandten Rolle. Andere Kombinationen aus Ausbildung und/oder Erfahrung können berücksichtigt werden. - Erfahrung in einem regulierten Umfeld der Biotechnologie, Pharmazeutik oder Lebenswissenschaften. - Starke Fähigkeit, in einer funktionsübergreifenden Matrixorganisation zu arbeiten. - Praktische Erfahrung mit Materialplanung, Logistikkoordination und operativer Umsetzung. - Starke analytische, organisatorische und Nachverfolgungsfähigkeiten. - Kenntnisse in ERP-Systemen, SharePoint und MS Office-Tools. - Fließend in Englisch. - Fließend in Deutsch wird bevorzugt; andere europäische Sprachen von Vorteil. Berichtet an: Associate Director, Lieferkette Standort: Visp, Schweiz (hybrid) oder Steinhausen, ZG, Schweiz (hybrid) Vergütung: Das Vergütungspaket ist wettbewerbsfähig und umfasst umfassende Leistungen sowie eine Aktienkomponente. #LI-JN1 Wir sind ein Arbeitgeber, der Chancengleichheit bietet. Alle qualifizierten Bewerber werden unabhängig von Rasse, Hautfarbe, Religion, Geschlecht, sexueller Orientierung, Geschlechtsidentität, nationaler Herkunft, Behinderung oder Veteranenstatus berücksichtigt.
descriptionByLocale.fr: Rejoignez notre mission pour protéger l'humanité ! Vaxcyte est une entreprise innovante dans le domaine des vaccins en phase clinique, qui développe des vaccins de haute fidélité pour protéger l'humanité des conséquences des maladies bactériennes, lesquelles peuvent avoir des répercussions graves et coûteuses sur la santé si elles ne sont pas contrôlées. Travailler à éradiquer ou traiter les infections bactériennes telles que la maladie pneumococcique invasive, le streptocoque du groupe A et la Shigella n'est que le début. Notre chemin vers le succès est clair et bien défini, et Vaxcyte est prête à aller jusqu'au bout. CE QUE nous faisons est tout aussi important que COMMENT nous le faisons ! Notre travail est guidé par quatre valeurs fondamentales et durables : *REPENSER LES CONVENTIONS : Nous apportons une diversité créative et intellectuelle à chaque aspect de notre travail afin d'innover et de réinventer la manière dont les vaccins sont délivrés. *VISER HAUT : Nous incarnons notre objectif collectif et audacieux de créer les biologiques les plus complexes jamais tentés pour protéger l'humanité. *DIRIGER AVEC CŒUR : Tout le monde chez Vaxcyte dirige avec une approche inclusive et bienveillante de la collaboration et du débat vigoureux qui fait avancer nos objectifs commerciaux. *MODÉLISER L'EXCELLENCE : L'ampleur de notre défi nécessite un engagement partagé à démontrer intégrité, responsabilité, égalité et clarté dans la communication et la prise de décision. ## Résumé : Le Responsable de la Gestion de la Chaîne d'Approvisionnement est chargé de l'exécution opérationnelle quotidienne des activités de la chaîne d'approvisionnement soutenant les programmes cliniques, GMP, PPQ et de préparation commerciale de Vaxcyte. Ce rôle se concentre sur la planification tactique, l'exécution, le suivi et la résolution des problèmes liés aux matériaux, à la logistique et aux partenaires externes, garantissant des opérations fluides et conformes pour soutenir les campagnes de fabrication. Ce rôle travaille en étroite collaboration avec le Directeur Associé de la Chaîne d'Approvisionnement, qui conserve la responsabilité stratégique globale des programmes, tandis que le Responsable de la Gestion de la Chaîne d'Approvisionnement assure une exécution disciplinée, un suivi et une stabilité opérationnelle. ## Fonctions Essentielles : ### Exécution Opérationnelle de la Chaîne d'Approvisionnement - Exécuter les stratégies et plans approuvés de la chaîne d'approvisionnement pour les activités GMP, PPQ et de préparation clinique/commerciale. - Gérer la planification, la commande et le suivi quotidiens des matériaux pour garantir leur disponibilité en temps voulu conformément aux calendriers de fabrication. - Soutenir l'exécution des activités d'approvisionnement sur plusieurs programmes, en mettant l'accent sur la préparation opérationnelle et la livraison. - Gérer et coordonner les Matières Premières Critiques et les Matériaux Fournis par le Client fournis à Lonza. ### Coordination des Matériaux, Logistique et Fournisseurs - Coordonner les expéditions et le stockage avec l'équipe Logistique. - Suivre les mouvements des matériaux, les inventaires et le statut des matériaux, en signalant de manière proactive les risques ou les retards, en particulier pour les Matières Premières Critiques. - Agir en tant qu'interface opérationnelle principale entre la Chaîne d'Approvisionnement et les Achats/Acheteurs, en garantissant l'alignement entre les matériaux commandés, les délais de livraison et les besoins d'exécution des campagnes. ### Support aux CDMO et Partenaires Externes - Soutenir les interfaces opérationnelles avec les CDMO, principalement Lonza. - Participer aux forums récurrents liés à Lonza (par exemple, réunions VIBEX, commandes de matériaux MINT, réunions d'allocation et d'alignement opérationnel). - Suivre les actions, les délais et la documentation requise pour permettre une exécution fluide. - Soutenir les activités liées aux déviations en coordonnant le flux d'informations entre la Chaîne d'Approvisionnement, la Qualité et les Opérations. ### Suivi, Reporting et Documentation - Maintenir des trackers opérationnels, des tableaux de bord et des mises à jour de statut liés aux matériaux, aux expéditions et à la préparation. - Assurer l'exactitude et la maintenance des données de progression des campagnes, y compris le suivi lié à OMP, en garantissant la cohérence entre la Chaîne d'Approvisionnement, la Fabrication et les données rapportées par Lonza. - Soutenir la préparation des mises à jour hebdomadaires et récurrentes des campagnes en fournissant des données d'exécution validées. ### Résolution de Problèmes et Amélioration Continue - Agir comme première ligne de réponse pour les problèmes opérationnels liés aux matériaux, à la logistique et à l'exécution. - Escalader les problèmes avec des données claires, des options et des recommandations au Directeur Associé. - Identifier les opportunités pour améliorer l'efficacité de l'exécution, le suivi et la robustesse opérationnelle. ## Exigences : - Diplôme de licence en Gestion de la Chaîne d'Approvisionnement, Opérations, Ingénierie, Sciences de la Vie ou un domaine connexe avec 7+ ans d'expérience dans les Opérations de la Chaîne d'Approvisionnement, le Support à la Fabrication ou un rôle connexe. D'autres combinaisons d'éducation et/ou d'expérience peuvent être envisagées. - Expérience dans un environnement réglementé en biotechnologie, pharmaceutique ou sciences de la vie. - Forte capacité à travailler dans une organisation matricielle et transversale. - Expérience pratique en planification des matériaux, coordination logistique et exécution opérationnelle. - Compétences analytiques, organisationnelles et de suivi solides. - Maîtrise des systèmes ERP, SharePoint et des outils MS Office. - Maîtrise de l'anglais. - La maîtrise de l'allemand est fortement préférée ; d'autres langues européennes sont un atout. Rapporte à : Directeur Associé, Chaîne d'Approvisionnement Lieu : Visp, Suisse (hybride) ou Steinhausen, ZG, Suisse (hybride) Rémunération : Le package de rémunération sera compétitif et inclut des avantages complets ainsi qu'une composante en actions. #LI-JN1 Nous sommes un employeur offrant l'égalité des chances. Tous les candidats qualifiés recevront une considération pour l'emploi sans distinction de race, couleur, religion, sexe, orientation sexuelle, identité de genre, origine nationale, handicap ou statut de vétéran.

jobs.description: Join our Mission to Protect Humankind! Vaxcyte is a clinical-stage vaccine innovation company engineering high-fidelity vaccines to protect humankind from the consequences of bacterial diseases, which have serious and costly health consequences when left unchecked. Working to eradicate or treat bacterial infections such as invasive pneumococcal disease, Group A Strep, and Shigella is just the beginning. Our path to success is clear and well-defined, and Vaxcyte is set up to go the distance. WHAT we do is every bit as important as HOW we do it! Our work together is guided by four enduring core values: *RETHINK CONVENTION: We bring creative and intellectual diversity to every facet of the work we do in order to innovate and re-innovate the way vaccines are delivered. *AIM HIGH: We embody our collectively audacious goal to courageously make the most complex biologics ever attempted to protect humankind. *LEAD WITH HEART: Everyone leads at Vaxcyte with a kindness-first, inclusive approach to collaboration and vigorous debate that advances our business objectives. *MODEL EXCELLENCE: The magnitude of our challenge requires our shared commitment to demonstrating integrity, accountability, equality and clarity across communications and decision making. ## Summary: Vaxcyte is seeking a Manager, Quality Assurance to support supplier oversight, audit management, and quality systems compliance. This role will lead and perform third-party audits, manage audit responses and CAPAs, and partner with Global Vendor Management and cross-functional teams to support supplier qualification, materials management, and quality agreements. The Manager, Quality Assurance will also contribute to the development and continuous improvement of quality systems and procedures to ensure compliance with cGMPs, regulatory requirements, and internal quality standards. ## Essential Functions: - Audit Program Management - Performs third-party audits, including notified bodies, vendor and contract manufacturing organization (CMO) audits, ensuring compliance with quality agreements and regulatory expectations. - Lead audit observations response and CAPA implementation, ensuring timely closure and effectiveness verification. - Collaborate with Global Vendor Management. - Suppliers and Materials Management - Involved in the process for new supplier’s introduction. - Involved in topics related to materials management such as VCNs (Vendor change notification). - Involved in the establishment of Quality Agreements and other required regulatory documentation according to cGMP. - Represent QA at internal and external cross functional team meetings related to supplier, services and/or materials. - Quality Systems and Compliance - Review and approve internal and external documentation for compliance such as deviations, risk assessments, change controls, CAPAs related to supplier, services and/or materials. - Develop, implement and maintain procedures and policies related to supplier, services and/or materials topics. - Participate in process improvement projects. - Interact with key stakeholders to develop solutions to complex issues while promoting quality standards of excellence. ## Requirements: - BS or BA with minimum 5 years of Quality Assurance experience. Other combinations of education and/or experience may be considered. - In-depth knowledge of GMPs, FDA/EMA regulations and ICH guidelines. - Strong attention to detail. - Significant auditing experience ((ASQ certification (CQA, CQE) or auditor certification preferred)). - Materials and suppliers’ management experience. - Excellent verbal, written, and interpersonal communication skills. - Experience leading and contributing through influence and working in cross functional teams. - Ability to work in a fast-paced team environment, and to consistently meet aggressive timelines while prioritizing tasks for multiple projects. - Ability to adapt to changing priorities. - Strong overall knowledge of biologics manufacturing processes and Quality Systems. - Ability to travel up to 40%. Reports to: Sr. Manager, Quality Assurance, Quality Management Systems Location: Visp, Switzerland Compensation: The compensation package will be competitive and includes comprehensive benefits and an equity component. We are an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability or veteran status.

The Manager, Quality Assurance will also contribute to the development and continuous improvement of quality systems and procedures to ensure compliance with cGMPs, regulatory requirements, and internal quality standards. ## Essential Functions: - Audit Program Management - Performs third-party audits, including notified bodies, vendor and contract manufacturing organization (CMO) audits, ensuring compliance with quality agreements and regulatory expectations. - Lead audit observations response and CAPA implementation, ensuring timely closure and effectiveness verification. - Collaborate with Global Vendor Management. - Suppliers and Materials Management - Involved in the process for new supplier’s introduction. - Involved in topics related to materials management such as VCNs (Vendor change notification). - Involved in the establishment of Quality Agreements and other required regulatory documentation according to cGMP. - Represent QA at internal and external cross functional team meetings related to supplier, services and/or materials. - Quality Systems and Compliance - Review and approve internal and external documentation for compliance such as deviations, risk assessments, change controls, CAPAs related to supplier, services and/or materials. - Develop, implement and maintain procedures and policies related to supplier, services and/or materials topics. - Participate in process improvement projects. - Interact with key stakeholders to develop solutions to complex issues while promoting quality standards of excellence. ## Requirements: - BS or BA with minimum 5 years of Quality Assurance experience. Other combinations of education and/or experience may be considered. - In-depth knowledge of GMPs, FDA/EMA regulations and ICH guidelines. - Strong attention to detail. - Significant auditing experience ((ASQ certification (CQA, CQE) or auditor certification preferred)). - Materials and suppliers’ management experience. - Excellent verbal, written, and interpersonal communication skills. - Experience leading and contributing through influence and working in cross functional teams. - Ability to work in a fast-paced team environment, and to consistently meet aggressive timelines while prioritizing tasks for multiple projects. - Ability to adapt to changing priorities. - Strong overall knowledge of biologics manufacturing processes and Quality Systems. - Ability to travel up to 40%. Reports to: Sr. Manager, Quality Assurance, Quality Management Systems Location: Visp, Switzerland Compensation: The compensation package will be competitive and includes comprehensive benefits and an equity component. We are an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability or veteran status.
descriptionByLocale.it: Unisciti alla nostra missione per proteggere l'umanità! Vaxcyte è un'azienda di vaccini in fase clinica che sviluppa vaccini ad alta fedeltà per proteggere l'umanità dalle conseguenze delle malattie batteriche, le quali hanno gravi e costose ripercussioni sulla salute se trascurate. Lavorare per debellare o trattare infezioni batteriche come la malattia pneumococcica invasiva, lo Strep di gruppo A e la Shigella è solo l’inizio. Il nostro percorso verso il successo è chiaro e ben definito, e Vaxcyte è pronta a percorrerlo fino in fondo. CIÒ che facciamo è importante tanto quanto COME lo facciamo! Il nostro lavoro è guidato da quattro valori fondamentali duraturi: *RIPENSARE LA CONVENZIONE: Portiamo diversità creativa e intellettuale in ogni aspetto del nostro lavoro per innovare e re-innovare il modo in cui vengono somministrati i vaccini. *MIRARE IN ALTO: Incarnamo il nostro obiettivo collettivo audace di produrre in modo coraggioso i biologic più complessi mai realizzati per proteggere l'umanità. *GUIDARE CON IL CUORE: Tutti guidano in Vaxcyte con un approccio inclusivo e basato sulla gentilezza nella collaborazione e nel dibattito vigoroso che promuove i nostri obiettivi aziendali. *MODELLARE L'ECCELLENZA: La portata della nostra sfida richiede il nostro impegno condiviso a dimostrare integrità, responsabilità, uguaglianza e chiarezza nelle comunicazioni e nelle decisioni. Sintesi: Vaxcyte cerca un Responsabile Assicurazione Qualità per supportare la supervisione dei fornitori, la gestione degli audit e la conformità dei sistemi di qualità. Questo ruolo guiderà e svolgerà audit di terze parti, gestirà le risposte agli audit e le CAPA, e collaborerà con la Gestione Fornitori Globale e le squadre cross-funzionali per supportare la qualificazione dei fornitori, la gestione dei materiali e gli accordi di qualità. Il Responsabile Assicurazione Qualità contribuirà inoltre allo sviluppo e al miglioramento continuo dei sistemi e delle procedure di qualità per garantire la conformità alle cGMP, ai requisiti normativi e agli standard interni di qualità. Funzioni essenziali: - Gestione del programma di audit - Esegue audit di terze parti, inclusi organismi notificati, audit di fornitori e organizzazioni di produzione su contratto (CMO), garantendo la conformità agli accordi di qualità e alle aspettative normative. - Guida le risposte alle osservazioni degli audit e l'implementazione delle CAPA, assicurando la chiusura tempestiva e la verifica dell'efficacia. - Collabora con la Gestione Fornitori Globale. - Gestione fornitori e materiali - Coinvolto nel processo di introduzione di nuovi fornitori. - Coinvolto in temi relativi alla gestione dei materiali come le VCN (notifiche di cambiamento del fornitore). - Coinvolto nell'istituzione di accordi di qualità e altra documentazione normativa richiesta secondo le cGMP. - Rappresenta l'Assicurazione Qualità in riunioni interne ed esterne di squadre cross-funzionali relative a fornitori, servizi e/o materiali. - Sistemi di qualità e conformità - Revisiona e approva documenti interni ed esterni per la conformità, come deviazioni, valutazioni del rischio, controlli di cambiamento e CAPA relativi a fornitori, servizi e/o materiali. - Sviluppa, implementa e mantiene procedure e politiche relative a fornitori, servizi e/o materiali. - Partecipa a progetti di miglioramento dei processi. - Interagisce con le parti interessate chiave per sviluppare soluzioni a problemi complessi promuovendo standard di eccellenza qualitativa. Requisiti: - Laurea triennale o magistrale con almeno 5 anni di esperienza in Assicurazione Qualità. Altre combinazioni di formazione ed esperienza potrebbero essere considerate. - Conoscenza approfondita delle GMP, delle normative FDA/EMA e delle linee guida ICH. - Forte attenzione ai dettagli. - Esperienza significativa in audit (preferibile certificazione ASQ (CQA, CQE) o certificazione auditor). - Esperienza nella gestione di materiali e fornitori. - Ottime capacità di comunicazione verbale, scritta e interpersonale. - Esperienza nel leadership e nel contribuire attraverso l'influenza lavorando in squadre cross-funzionali. - Capacità di lavorare in un ambiente di squadra dinamico e di rispettare scadenze aggressive, priorizzando i compiti per più progetti. - Capacità di adattarsi a priorità mutevoli. - Conoscenza complessiva dei processi di produzione di biologic e dei Sistemi di Qualità. - Disponibilità a viaggiare fino al 40%. Relazione gerarchica: Senior Manager, Assicurazione Qualità, Sistemi di Gestione Qualità Località: Visp, Svizzera Retribuzione: La retribuzione sarà competitiva e include benefit completi e una componente azionaria. Siamo un datore di lavoro che offre pari opportunità. Tutti i candidati qualificati saranno considerati per l'impiego senza distinzione di razza, colore, religione, sesso, orientamento sessuale, identità di genere, origine nazionale, disabilità o status di veterano.
descriptionByLocale.de: Treten Sie unserer Mission bei, die Menschheit zu schützen! Vaxcyte ist ein Unternehmen in der klinischen Phase, das innovative Impfstoffe entwickelt, um die Menschheit vor den Folgen bakterieller Erkrankungen zu schützen – diese haben schwerwiegende und kostspielige gesundheitliche Folgen, wenn sie unbehandelt bleiben. Die Bekämpfung oder Behandlung von bakteriellen Infektionen wie invasiven Pneumokokken-Erkrankungen, Gruppe-A-Streptokokken und Shigella ist erst der Anfang. Unser Weg zum Erfolg ist klar definiert, und Vaxcyte ist bereit, diesen Weg bis zum Ende zu gehen. WAS wir tun, ist genauso wichtig wie WIE wir es tun! Unsere Arbeit wird von vier grundlegenden Werten geleitet: *GEWOHNHEITEN NEU DENKEN: Wir bringen kreative und intellektuelle Vielfalt in jeden Aspekt unserer Arbeit ein, um Impfstoffe neu zu erfinden und weiterzuentwickeln. *HOCH ZIELEN: Wir verkörpern unser gemeinsames, ehrgeiziges Ziel, die komplexesten Biologika herzustellen, um die Menschheit zu schützen. *MIT HERZ FÜHREN: Bei Vaxcyte führt jeder mit einem inklusiven, freundlichen Ansatz in der Zusammenarbeit und lebhaften Debatten, die unsere Geschäftsziele voranbringen. *EXZELLENZ VORLEBEN: Die Größe unserer Herausforderung erfordert unser gemeinsames Engagement für Integrität, Verantwortung, Gleichheit und Klarheit in Kommunikation und Entscheidungsfindung. Zusammenfassung: Vaxcyte sucht eine/n Manager/in Qualitätssicherung zur Unterstützung der Lieferantenüberwachung, Auditverwaltung und Einhaltung von Qualitätssystemen. Diese Rolle leitet und führt Drittanbieter-Audits durch, verwaltet Auditantworten und CAPAs und arbeitet mit dem globalen Lieferantenmanagement sowie funktionsübergreifenden Teams zusammen, um die Qualifizierung von Lieferanten, das Materialmanagement und Qualitätsvereinbarungen zu unterstützen. Der/die Manager/in Qualitätssicherung trägt zudem zur Entwicklung und kontinuierlichen Verbesserung von Qualitätssystemen und -verfahren bei, um die Einhaltung von cGMPs, regulatorischen Anforderungen und internen Qualitätsstandards sicherzustellen. Kernaufgaben: - Auditprogramm-Management - Durchführung von Drittanbieter-Audits, einschließlich Audits von benannten Stellen, Lieferanten und Auftragsherstellern (CMO), um die Einhaltung von Qualitätsvereinbarungen und regulatorischen Erwartungen sicherzustellen. - Leitung der Beantwortung von Auditbeobachtungen und Umsetzung von CAPAs, um eine zeitnahe Schließung und Wirksamkeitsprüfung zu gewährleisten. - Zusammenarbeit mit dem globalen Lieferantenmanagement. - Lieferanten- und Materialmanagement - Beteiligung am Prozess der Einführung neuer Lieferanten. - Bearbeitung von Themen im Zusammenhang mit dem Materialmanagement, z. B. VCNs (Lieferantenänderungsmeldungen). - Mitwirkung bei der Erstellung von Qualitätsvereinbarungen und anderer erforderlicher regulatorischer Dokumentation gemäß cGMP. - Vertretung der Qualitätssicherung in internen und externen funktionsübergreifenden Teamsitzungen zu Lieferanten, Dienstleistungen und/oder Materialien. - Qualitätssysteme und Compliance - Prüfung und Freigabe interner und externer Dokumente auf Compliance, z. B. Abweichungen, Risikobewertungen, Änderungssteuerungen und CAPAs im Zusammenhang mit Lieferanten, Dienstleistungen und/oder Materialien. - Entwicklung, Implementierung und Pflege von Verfahren und Richtlinien zu Lieferanten, Dienstleistungen und/oder Materialien. - Teilnahme an Prozessverbesserungsprojekten. - Interaktion mit wichtigen Stakeholdern zur Entwicklung von Lösungen für komplexe Probleme unter Förderung von Qualitätsstandards. Anforderungen: - Bachelor- oder gleichwertiger Abschluss mit mindestens 5 Jahren Erfahrung in Qualitätssicherung. Andere Kombinationen aus Ausbildung und/oder Erfahrung können berücksichtigt werden. - Fundierte Kenntnisse der GMPs, FDA/EMA-Vorschriften und ICH-Richtlinien. - Hohe Detailgenauigkeit. - Umfangreiche Audit-Erfahrung (ASQ-Zertifizierung (CQA, CQE) oder Auditor-Zertifizierung bevorzugt). - Erfahrung im Management von Materialien und Lieferanten. - Hervorragende mündliche, schriftliche und zwischenmenschliche Kommunikationsfähigkeiten. - Erfahrung in der Führung und Einflussnahme in funktionsübergreifenden Teams. - Fähigkeit, in einem dynamischen Teamumfeld zu arbeiten und gleichzeitig aggressive Zeitpläne einzuhalten sowie Aufgaben für mehrere Projekte zu priorisieren. - Anpassungsfähigkeit an sich ändernde Prioritäten. - Umfassende Kenntnisse der Herstellungsprozesse für Biologika und Qualitätssysteme. - Bereitschaft zu Reisen von bis zu 40%. Berichtsweg: Senior Manager, Qualitätssicherung, Qualitätsmanagementsysteme Standort: Visp, Schweiz Vergütung: Das Vergütungspaket ist wettbewerbsfähig und umfasst umfassende Benefits sowie eine Beteiligung am Eigenkapital. Wir sind ein Arbeitgeber, der Chancengleichheit bietet. Alle qualifizierten Bewerber:innen werden ohne Rücksicht auf Rasse, Hautfarbe, Religion, Geschlecht, sexuelle Orientierung, nationale Herkunft, Behinderung oder Veteranenstatus berücksichtigt.
descriptionByLocale.fr: Rejoignez notre mission pour protéger l’humanité ! Vaxcyte est une entreprise de vaccins en phase clinique qui conçoit des vaccins de haute fidélité pour protéger l’humanité des conséquences des maladies bactériennes, lesquelles ont de graves et coûteuses répercussions sur la santé si elles ne sont pas maîtrisées. Travailler à l’éradication ou au traitement d’infections bactériennes telles que les maladies pneumococciques invasives, le streptocoque du groupe A et la shigellose n’est que le début. Notre chemin vers le succès est clair et bien défini, et Vaxcyte est prêt à aller jusqu’au bout. CE que nous faisons est tout aussi important que la MANIÈRE dont nous le faisons ! Notre travail est guidé par quatre valeurs fondamentales durables : *REPENSER LES CONVENTIONS : Nous apportons une diversité créative et intellectuelle à chaque facette de notre travail afin d’innover et de réinnover la manière dont les vaccins sont administrés. *VISER HAUT : Nous incarnons notre objectif collectif audacieux de produire courageusement les biologiques les plus complexes jamais tentés pour protéger l’humanité. *DIRIGER AVEC LE CŒUR : Chacun dirige chez Vaxcyte avec une approche inclusive et bienveillante dans la collaboration et les débats vigoureux qui font avancer nos objectifs commerciaux. *MODÉLISER L’EXCELLENCE : L’ampleur de notre défi exige notre engagement partagé à démontrer intégrité, responsabilité, égalité et clarté dans les communications et la prise de décision. Résumé : Vaxcyte recherche un Responsable Assurance Qualité pour soutenir la supervision des fournisseurs, la gestion des audits et la conformité des systèmes qualité. Ce poste supervisera et réalisera des audits de tierces parties, gérera les réponses aux audits et les CAPA, et collaborera avec la Gestion Globale des Fournisseurs ainsi qu’avec les équipes transversales pour soutenir la qualification des fournisseurs, la gestion des matériaux et les accords qualité. Le Responsable Assurance Qualité contribuera également au développement et à l’amélioration continue des systèmes et procédures qualité afin de garantir la conformité aux cGMP, aux exigences réglementaires et aux normes qualité internes. Fonctions essentielles : - Gestion du programme d’audit - Réalisation d’audits de tierces parties, y compris des audits d’organismes notifiés, de fournisseurs et d’organisations de fabrication sous contrat (CMO), en veillant au respect des accords qualité et des attentes réglementaires. - Pilotage des réponses aux observations d’audit et mise en œuvre des CAPA, en assurant leur clôture en temps opportun et la vérification de leur efficacité. - Collaboration avec la Gestion Globale des Fournisseurs. - Gestion des fournisseurs et des matériaux - Participation au processus d’introduction de nouveaux fournisseurs. - Intervention sur des sujets liés à la gestion des matériaux, tels que les VCN (notifications de changement de fournisseur). - Participation à l’établissement d’accords qualité et d’autres documents réglementaires requis selon les cGMP. - Représentation de l’Assurance Qualité lors de réunions internes et externes d’équipes transversales concernant les fournisseurs, services et/ou matériaux. - Systèmes qualité et conformité - Révision et approbation de documents internes et externes pour la conformité, tels que les écarts, évaluations des risques, contrôles de changement et CAPA liés aux fournisseurs, services et/ou matériaux. - Développement, mise en œuvre et maintenance de procédures et politiques relatives aux fournisseurs, services et/ou matériaux. - Participation à des projets d’amélioration des processus. - Interaction avec les parties prenantes clés pour développer des solutions à des problèmes complexes tout en promouvant des normes d’excellence qualité. Exigences : - Licence ou équivalent avec au moins 5 ans d’expérience en Assurance Qualité. D’autres combinaisons de formation et/ou d’expérience peuvent être prises en considération. - Connaissance approfondie des BPF, des réglementations FDA/EMA et des lignes directrices ICH. - Grande attention aux détails. - Expérience significative en audit (certification ASQ (CQA, CQE) ou certification d’auditeur préférée). - Expérience en gestion des matériaux et des fournisseurs. - Excellentes compétences en communication verbale, écrite et interpersonnelle. - Expérience en leadership et contribution par l’influence au sein d’équipes transversales. - Capacité à travailler dans un environnement d’équipe dynamique et à respecter des échéances agressives tout en priorisant les tâches pour plusieurs projets. - Capacité à s’adapter à des priorités changeantes. - Connaissance globale des processus de fabrication de biologiques et des Systèmes Qualité. - Disponibilité à voyager jusqu’à 40%. Lien hiérarchique : Senior Manager, Assurance Qualité, Systèmes de Gestion de la Qualité Lieu : Visp, Suisse Rémunération : Le package de rémunération sera compétitif et comprendra des avantages sociaux complets ainsi qu’une composante en actions. Nous sommes un employeur garantissant l’égalité des chances. Tous les candidats qualifiés seront pris en considération pour l’emploi sans distinction de race, de couleur, de religion, de sexe, d’orientation sexuelle, d’identité de genre, de nationalité, de handicap ou de statut de vétéran.

jobs.title: Director, Business Operations (Global Quality Control)
jobs.description: Join our Mission to Protect Humankind! Vaxcyte is a clinical-stage vaccine innovation company engineering high-fidelity vaccines to protect humankind from the consequences of bacterial diseases, which have serious and costly health consequences when left unchecked. Working to eradicate or treat bacterial infections such as invasive pneumococcal disease, Group A Strep, and Shigella is just the beginning. Our path to success is clear and well-defined, and Vaxcyte is set up to go the distance. WHAT we do is every bit as important as HOW we do it! Our work together is guided by four enduring core values: *RETHINK CONVENTION: We bring creative and intellectual diversity to every facet of the work we do in order to innovate and re-innovate the way vaccines are delivered. *AIM HIGH: We embody our collectively audacious goal to courageously make the most complex biologics ever attempted to protect humankind. *LEAD WITH HEART: Everyone leads at Vaxcyte with a kindness-first, inclusive approach to collaboration and vigorous debate that advances our business objectives. *MODEL EXCELLENCE: The magnitude of our challenge requires our shared commitment to demonstrating integrity, accountability, equality and clarity across communications and decision making. ## Summary: The Director, Business Operations (Global QC) will lead the strategic design, implementation, and continuous optimization of global operational frameworks supporting Vaxcyte’s Quality Control (QC) and supporting Analytical interfaces. This role ensures that analytical inputs and QC operations are scalable, compliant, efficient, and aligned with corporate and regulatory expectations as the organization advances from development through commercial readiness. The Director will serve as the central business operations leader for the Global Quality Control, partnering with leaders in Analytical Development, Analytical Science & Technology, Stability, Process Development, Manufacturing Science and Technology (MS&T), and Quality. This individual will drive governance, process standardization, infrastructure development, and cross-functional coordination to enable timely, compliant, and uninterrupted supply of Vaxcyte’s innovative vaccines. ## Essential Functions: Operational Excellence & Strategy • Lead the design and execution of operational strategies that translate Vaxcyte’s global vision into efficient, compliant, and scalable QC and analytical business operations. • Develop and maintain a global QC operations strategy, ensuring alignment with clinical and commercial objectives. • Implement continuous improvement frameworks (e.g., Plan-Do-Check-Act) to enhance efficiency, scalability, and compliance across analytical and QC processes. • Define and monitor key performance indicators (KPIs) that assess operational performance, quality, delivery timelines, and cost efficiency across the global analytics network. • Partner with cross-functional leaders to ensure analytical and QC infrastructure, systems, and processes are integrated into overall business objectives. • Lead initiatives that improve operational automation, data visibility, and process robustness. Governance & Compliance • Establish and maintain robust governance frameworks for QC and analytical operations in alignment with global cGMP regulations and internal quality standards. • Define, visualize, and formalize QC business processes using RASCI principles, ensuring clear ownership and accountability across the analytical network. • Review and enhance the Quality Management System (QMS) infrastructure to close gaps, harmonize SOPs, and strengthen procedural consistency. • Ensure data integrity principles are embedded in all operational practices, compliant with Vaxcyte’s DI policies and global standards. • Support global inspection readiness and lead preparations for regulatory inspections, partner audits, and customer reviews. • Collaborate with Quality to ensure analytical operations and contract testing laboratories meet all compliance requirements. Infrastructure & Systems Leadership • Oversee the development and validation of business systems, data management platforms, and documentation practices across global analytical and QC sites. • Ensure that contract and in-house laboratories maintain robust equipment qualification, calibration, and maintenance programs to support reliable analytical testing. • Lead network and site planning activities, including external partnerships and outsourcing strategies with CDMOs and CROs. • Develop business cases for laboratory expansion, outsourcing scenarios, and infrastructure investments to support strategic growth. Capability Building & Team Leadership • Collaborate and mentor a global team of QC operations professionals to ensure scientific, technical, and operational excellence. • Drive training programs that enhance technical proficiency, compliance culture, and cross-functional understanding within the analytics organization. • Foster a culture of innovation, learning, and continuous improvement across the analytical network. • Collaborate closely with functional heads across Analytical Development, Analytical Science and Technology, Stability, and Manufacturing to harmonize operational practices. • Represent Global Analytics and QC Operations in governance bodies, steering committees, and cross-functional business forums. ## Requirements: • Bachelor’s or Master’s degree (or higher) in Biotechnology, Pharmaceutical Sciences, Engineering, or related field. • 12+ years of progressive experience in Quality Control, Analytical Operations, or Technical Operations within a biotechnology or pharmaceutical company. • Demonstrated leadership in developing and managing operational systems in GMP-regulated environments. • Strong understanding of cGMP, global regulatory requirements, and analytical testing operations. • Proven ability to design and implement business processes, governance models, and performance management systems. • Experience with vendor management and collaboration with CDMOs/CROs on analytical testing and QC operations. • Exceptional communication, negotiation, and relationship-building skills with internal and external stakeholders. • Strong analytical, organizational, and problem-solving skills; adept at data-driven decision-making. • Demonstrated passion for process improvement, operational efficiency, and automation Reports to: Head of Quality Control Location: Visp, Switzerland Compensation: The compensation package will be competitive and includes an equity component. Relocation: This role is not eligible for relocation assistance. We are an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability or veteran status.
